content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Each element of AAA is 0,1,0, 1, 0,1, or 222.

You can perform the following operation as many times as you like:


Choose an index iii (1‚â§i<‚à£A‚à£1 \leq i < |A|1‚â§i<‚à£A‚à£), delete AiA_iAi‚Äã and Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1‚Äã from the array, and insert (Ai+Ai+1)‚Äämod‚Ää3(A_i + A_{i+1}) \bmod 3(Ai‚Äã+Ai+1‚Äã)mod3 in their place.

Note that this operation reduces the length of AAA by 111.
Define f(A)f(A)f(A) to be the lexicographically minimum‚ÄÝ^\dagger‚ÄÝ possible array that AAA can be transformed into using this operation several (possibly, zero) times.

Also define val(A)\text{val}(A)val(A) to be the ternary value of the array AAA. Formally,

val(A)=‚àëi=1NAi‚ãÖ3i‚àí1\text{val}(A) = \sum_{i=1}^N A_i \cdot 3^{i-1}val(A)=i=1‚àëN‚ÄãAi‚Äã‚ãÖ3i‚àí1Your task is to compute

‚àëi=1N‚àëj=iNval(f(A[i‚Ä¶j]))\sum_{i=1}^N \sum_{j=i}^N \text{val}(f(A[i\ldots j]))i=1‚àëN‚Äãj=i‚àëN‚Äãval(f(A[i‚Ä¶j]))where A[i‚Ä¶j]A[i\ldots j]A[i‚Ä¶j] denotes the subarray of AAA from index iii to index jjj, i.e. the array [Ai,Ai+1,‚Ä¶,Aj][A_i, A_{i+1}, \ldots, A_j][Ai‚Äã,Ai+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Aj‚Äã].

The answer can be large, so compute it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

‚ÄÝ^\dagger‚ÄÝ An array PPP is said to be lexicographically smaller than another array QQQ if and only if one of the following holds:


PPP is a prefix of QQQ, but P‚âÝQP \neq QPÓÄÝ=Q; or
There exists an index iii such that Pi<QiP_i < Q_iPi‚Äã<Qi‚Äã, and Pj=QjP_j = Q_jPj‚Äã=Qj‚Äã for all 1‚â§j<i1 \leq j < i1‚â§j<i.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚Äî the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the value of  ‚àëi=1N‚àëj=iNval(f(A[i‚Ä¶j]))\ \displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^N \sum_{j=i}^N \text{val}(f(A[i\ldots j])) i=1‚àëN‚Äãj=i‚àëN‚Äãval(f(A[i‚Ä¶j])), modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We have the following subarrays:

[2][2][2], for which f([2])=[2]f([2]) = [2]f([2])=[2] has a value of 2‚ãÖ30=22\cdot 3^0 = 22‚ãÖ30=2.
[1][1][1], for which f([1])=[1]f([1]) = [1]f([1])=[1] has a value of 1‚ãÖ30=11\cdot 3^0 = 11‚ãÖ30=1.
[2,1][2, 1][2,1], for which f([2,1])=[0]f([2, 1]) = [0]f([2,1])=[0] has a value of 0‚ãÖ30=00\cdot 3^0 = 00‚ãÖ30=0.

The sum of all values is 2+1+0=32+1+0 = 32+1+0=3.
